title: genista infesta
intro: Genista infesta, also known as broom or Scotch broom, is a shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and small, green leaves. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating rheumatism, and supporting liver function. In herbal preparations, Genista infesta is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
